questions.question: Who is Bholi?
questions.correct_answer: A simple and neglected girl whose real name is Sulekha
questions.explanation: Bholi is called a simpleton due to her stammer and lack of confidence caused by neglect and discrimination.

questions.question: Why was Bholi considered different from other children?
questions.correct_answer: She stammered and had pock-marks on her face
questions.explanation: A childhood illness left her physically scarred and emotionally weak.

questions.question: Why did Bholi’s parents send her to school?
questions.correct_answer: Because they thought she had little chance of marriage
questions.explanation: Bholi was sent to school not out of concern but due to social pressure.
questions.source_section: Decision to send Bholi to school

questions.question: Who played a major role in changing Bholi’s life?
questions.explanation: The teacher’s kindness and encouragement help Bholi gain confidence.

questions.question: How did the teacher help Bholi overcome her fear?
questions.correct_answer: By speaking kindly and encouraging her to speak
questions.explanation: Positive reinforcement helped Bholi gain self-confidence.

questions.question: Why did Bholi initially agree to the marriage proposal?
questions.explanation: She was ready to sacrifice her happiness for her family’s reputation.

questions.question: Why did Bholi refuse to marry Bishamber Nath?
questions.correct_answer: He demanded dowry and showed greed
questions.explanation: Bholi realised her self-worth and rejected injustice.

questions.question: What change is seen in Bholi at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Education transforms Bholi into a bold and independent person.

questions.question: What social evil is highlighted in the story?
questions.explanation: The story criticises unfair treatment of women and dowry demands.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story ‘Bholi’?
questions.explanation: True empowerment comes through education and self-belief.

notes.title: Role of Education
notes.content: Education transforms Bholi from a fearful girl into a confident woman.

notes.title: Teacher’s Influence
notes.content: The teacher’s encouragement plays a key role in Bholi’s development.

facts.fact: The story strongly condemns the dowry system.
